title: The Better Angels of Our Nature
author: Steven Pinker
content: The most important lesson from the history of violence is that we have been getting better. The decline of violence is a fractal phenomenon, visible at many scales: the scale of millennia, centuries, decades, and years. It applies to many kinds of violence: homicide, war, genocide, torture, and the treatment of children, homosexuals, racial and ethnic minorities, and animals. The forces of civilization, reason, and empathy have been defeating the forces of barbarism. This is not a story of inevitability, but of human accomplishment. It is not a story of a steady moral improvement, but of a waxing and waning of violence that has trended downward over the long term. The better angels of our nature are the forces that have driven this decline: empathy, self-control, moral sense, and reason.
questions_raised: Is human progress inevitable or contingent?
How do empathy and reason reduce violence?
What role can technology play in human betterment?
insights_gained: Human civilization has shown long-term progress
Empathy and reason can overcome barbarism
Progress requires active cultivation of better instincts
